description: Search the GRC Migrate directory of SOC 2 audit and compliance-readiness firms (facts compiled from public sources). Filter by framework, ecosystem, partner status, or category. Read-only.

description.name: get_firm
description.description: Get one firm's public directory record by slug. Returns null for unknown or non-public (suppressed) firms.

description.name: get_introduction_path
description.description: Get the public directory URL and what an introduction to a firm requires. READ-ONLY: returns instructions only — it does not submit a request or create a lead.

description.name: list_resources
description.description: List GRC Migrate guides, question pages, and blog posts available to read (title, description, url, type). Read-only.

description.name: get_resource
description.description: Get one guide, question page, or blog post by slug — its title, description, and content (blog body markdown, or the FAQ question/answer pairs for guides and question pages).

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_introduction_path, list_resources, get_resource · Private data: get_firm · Public sink: get_introduction_path, get_resource.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_introduction_path, list_resources, get_resource · Private data: get_firm · Public sink: get_introduction_path, get_resource

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
